summary: Validated new /api/admin/orders/manual-create endpoint (8 backend tests inc. validation, happy paths, stock 409, explicit links, custom qty pricing, auth gate) + existing admin endpoints regression (4 tests) + Stripe Checkout end-to-end (real checkout.stripe.com URL returned) + Admin UI manual order + manual link forms via Playwright. 12/12 backend tests pass. UI flows succeed with success banners and DB persistence visible. One cosmetic frontend bug: orders table renders price as raw escape '5\u20ac' instead of '5€'.

ui_bugs.component: AdminDashboard orders table (Commandes tab, Montant column)
ui_bugs.issue: Price renders as literal escape sequence '5\u20ac', '52.5\u20ac', '60\u20ac' instead of '5€', '52.50€', '60€'. Likely a string with escaped backslash (e.g. "\\u20ac") instead of the actual € character or unescaped "\u20ac". Search /app/frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.js for '\\u20ac' or 'u20ac'. Cosmetic only — values are numerically correct and the form/inputs themselves render € properly elsewhere on the page (e.g. pack dropdown 'Starter · 1 lien · 5€' is fine).
ui_bugs.selector: Commandes tab orders table > Montant column cells

action_items: (LOW) Fix orders-table currency rendering in AdminDashboard.js — replace escaped '\\u20ac' with '€' (or unescape \u20ac) in the Montant column cell. Affects only the admin Commandes table display.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend manual-create endpoint is well-structured: validates email, resolves pack vs custom quantity correctly, pre-checks stock with proper 409, supports explicit-links bypass, marks links sold + binds order_id, awards loyalty points, sends email only when status completed AND assigned_links non-empty. Mongo _id is correctly stripped after insert_one (line 3412). Status transitions: pending→completed when full assignment, partial when some, pending when none.

Stripe create-session endpoint correctly persists payment_transactions BEFORE returning the URL and saves payment_url on the order — good fallback design. Verified end-to-end: returns real https://checkout.stripe.com/g/pay/cs_live_... URL.
Admin login cookie is samesite=none + secure (line 1533) — good for cross-site preview env; works with Playwright/browser cookie jar.

AdminDashboard.js is 1009 lines — over the 700-line guideline. Manual-order section (~180 lines, 681-820) and manual-links section (~80 lines) could be extracted into /components/admin/ManualOrderForm.jsx + ManualLinksForm.jsx for maintainability.
Manual order form correctly defaults to fr language and posts via axios with withCredentials — consistent with rest of admin panel.
Stock pre-check logic at line 3381 correctly excluded when explicit links provided (uses 'and not req.links') — prevents false 409 when admin supplies own links.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on admin manual order + manual link flows (Stripe checkout redirect not browser-tested due to LIVE-mode constraint, but backend-verified to return real Stripe URL)

seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_-prefixed orders and TEST_/e2e_-prefixed links during testing. Visible in orders table (e.g. test_manual_explicit, test_manual_custom_15, manual_e2e). These can be cleaned via the trash icons in the admin UI or by deleting orders prefixed with TEST_/manual_/stripe_redirect_test in db.orders.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile regression items (FAQ accordion, carousels, /offers buy buttons) were NOT re-tested in this iteration since iteration_11 already validated them and there was no code change in those areas per the request. Backend manual-create tests are stable & idempotent (use uuid suffixes). Stripe end-to-end was verified via curl using a freshly-created pending manual order — the response includes a live checkout.stripe.com URL. DO NOT actually pay on Stripe.
rca of the issue: Cosmetic '5\u20ac' rendering: the order list cell appears to use a JSON-escaped euro string rather than the literal char. Likely fixable by replacing '\\u20ac' → '€' (or '\u20ac' literal) in the row template inside AdminDashboard.js. Reproduction: admin → Commandes → look at any row in the Montant column. The pack dropdown a few px above renders € correctly, so the bug is localized to the orders-table render.
